description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just been handed three large application
  log-files that intermittently throw errors.  
  Management wants a one-shot shell automation that does two things in
  parallel:

  1.  Generates a *deterministic* error-summary report that aggregates **only**
      the lines that contain the literal string “ERROR”.
  2.  Compresses each original log file so it can be archived.

  The existing, uncompressed log files are already present in your home
  directory:

      /home/user/logs/app1.log
      /home/user/logs/app2.log
      /home/user/logs/app3.log

  Your tasks

  A.  Create the directory /home/user/tools (if it does not already exist)
      and place inside it a POSIX-compliant shell script named
      /home/user/tools/error_summary.sh

      • The script **must** work when it is invoked with zero arguments.  
        When no arguments are provided it must automatically scan every
        “*.log” file located in /home/user/logs.  
        (When one or more file paths are passed, it should scan only those
        paths.)

      • For each log file, it must spawn a background subshell that:
          1. Extracts **exactly** the lines that contain the substring
             “ERROR” (match is case sensitive).
          2. Counts how many error lines are in that file.
          3. Counts how many times each *distinct* error message occurs
             (a “distinct error message” is the entire text after the time
             stamp).  
             Hints: cut/awk/sed are fine; you may assume the timestamp ends
             after the first closing square-bracket “] ”.

        All these per-file subshells must run simultaneously and the parent
        script must `wait` for them before continuing.

      • After all subshells finish, the parent script must assemble a single
        report file called:

            /home/user/logs/error_summary.log

        The report must be overwritten on every run.

      • REQUIRED output format that the grading system will check
        (use literally the same spacing and keywords):

            Error Summary Report
            Generated: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

            File: app1.log
            Total Errors: <integer>
              - <error text A> (<count>)
              - <error text B> (<count>)
            <blank line>
            File: app2.log
            Total Errors: <integer>
              - <error text> (<count>)
            <blank line>
            File: app3.log
            Total Errors: <integer>
            <blank line>
            Overall Total Errors: <integer>

        1.  The first line is a fixed literal.  
        2.  The second line must contain the wall-clock time at script start,
            in the exact format returned by  
            `date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"`.
        3.  The file sections **must** appear in alphabetic order by base
            filename (app1.log, app2.log, app3.log).  
        4.  “Total Errors:” value is the count of “ERROR” lines in *that*
            log.  
        5.  Each “ - ” (dash, space) bullet immediately follows two spaces.
            After the dash there is a single space, the full original error
            message, a single space, and the count in parentheses.  
            The unique error messages for a file must be sorted
            alphabetically.  
        6.  A single completely blank line must exist between sections.  
        7.  The very last line of the report is  
            “Overall Total Errors: N”  
            where N is the sum of all per-file totals.

      • The script must exit with status 0 when it succeeds and non-zero
        otherwise.

  B.  Parallel compression

      After generating the report, the same parent shell script must kick off
      gzip compression of every log it processed (again, via background
      subshells) and move the resulting “.gz” files into a directory

          /home/user/archive/

      The directory must be created by the script if necessary.  Do **not**
      delete the original “.log” files.

  C.  Make the script executable (`chmod +x`).

  Verification hint  
  Once finished, you can run

      /home/user/tools/error_summary.sh

  and you should see /home/user/logs/error_summary.log appear, and the “.gz”
  archives accumulate under /home/user/archive/.

  Note: You do **not** have root privileges; rely only on common POSIX tools
  available in a standard minimal container image (bash, grep, awk, sed,
  cut, sort, gzip, etc.).  You are free to create helper functions or
  temporary files inside /tmp but they must be cleaned up before the script
  returns.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth state before agent starts
  -->
  <initialFiles>
    <file path="/home/user/logs/app1.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-08-01 10:00:00] INFO Starting service
[2023-08-01 10:05:00] ERROR Failed to connect to database
[2023-08-01 10:06:00] WARN Cache miss
[2023-08-01 10:07:00] ERROR Timeout while reading from socket
[2023-08-01 10:08:00] INFO Service running
[2023-08-01 10:09:00] ERROR Failed to connect to database
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/logs/app2.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-08-01 09:00:00] INFO Booting
[2023-08-01 09:01:00] ERROR NullPointerException at line 42
[2023-08-01 09:02:00] ERROR NullPointerException at line 42
[2023-08-01 09:03:00] INFO Ready
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/logs/app3.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-08-01 11:00:00] INFO Starting cron
[2023-08-01 11:01:00] WARN Slow query
[2023-08-01 11:02:00] INFO Tick
]]></file>
  </initialFiles>

  <!--
    Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes successfully
  -->
  <expectedArtefacts>
    <!-- Script -->
    <file path="/home/user/tools/error_summary.sh" mustExist="true" mustBeExecutable="true"/>
    <!-- Report -->
    <file path="/home/user/logs/error_summary.log"><!--
      Timestamp is variable and therefore not asserted verbatim by the test
      suite.  Everything else must match.
    --></file>
    <!-- Archives -->
    <file path="/home/user/archive/app1.log.gz"/>
    <file path="/home/user/archive/app2.log.gz"/>
    <file path="/home/user/archive/app3.log.gz"/>
  </expectedArtefacts>

  <!--
    Deterministic contents that the test harness *will* assert line-by-line,
    ignoring only the timestamp line.
  -->
  <expectedReport><![CDATA[
Error Summary Report
Generated: IGNORE_TIMESTAMP

File: app1.log
Total Errors: 3
  - ERROR Failed to connect to database (2)
  - ERROR Timeout while reading from socket (1)

File: app2.log
Total Errors: 2
  - ERROR NullPointerException at line 42 (2)

File: app3.log
Total Errors: 0

Overall Total Errors: 5
]]></expectedReport>
